summary: Matched the common grounded side-special input/entry logic by moving three routines out of asm/melee/ft/ft_08A4.s into new C source src/melee/ft/chara/ftCommon/ftCo_SpecialS.c. The PR names ft_800964FC as ftCo_SpecialS_HasInput, ft_80096540 as ftCo_SpecialS_CheckInput, and factors the state-entry work into static doEnter. It also wires the new object into obj_files.mk, updates callers from the old address-style names, adds focused headers for ftCo_SpecialS and nearby 0961xx fall-special helpers, corrects several field/macro types needed for the match, and reports 100% for ftCo_SpecialS.c.
what_changed: Removed the asm implementations for ft_800964FC, ft_80096540, and ftCo_800965DC from asm/melee/ft/ft_08A4.s and replaced their callsites with named C symbols where visible.
Added src/melee/ft/chara/ftCommon/ftCo_SpecialS.c with ftCo_SpecialS_HasInput, ftCo_SpecialS_CheckInput, and static doEnter.
ftCo_SpecialS_HasInput checks B input via fp->input.x668 & HSD_PAD_B and horizontal stick magnitude against p_ftCommonData->x218 without entering the state.

ftCo_SpecialS_CheckInput gates on ftData_SpecialS[fp->kind] being non-NULL and fp->x688 being zero, turns the fighter around with ftCommon_8007D9FC when stick direction opposes facing_dir past -p_ftCommonData->x220, then enters the character-specific SpecialS callback.
doEnter applies ground friction adjustment to fp->gr_vel using fp->co_attrs.xB8 and ft_GetGroundFrictionMultiplier(fp), then calls ftData_SpecialS[fp->kind](gobj).

Added src/melee/ft/chara/ftCommon/ftCo_SpecialS.h for the two public SpecialS helpers, and added src/melee/ft/chara/ftCommon/ftCo_0961.h for nearby ft_8009665C/ft_800968C8/ft_80096900/ftCo_FallSpecial_* declarations that had previously lived in broader headers.
Updated many IASA and character-special files to include ftCo_SpecialS.h or ftCo_0961.h and to call ftCo_SpecialS_CheckInput instead of ft_80096540.
Updated fighter.c to call ftCo_SpecialS_HasInput instead of ft_800964FC when maintaining fp->x688.
Declared ftData_SpecialS, ftData_SpecialAirHi, and ftData_SpecialAirLw in ftdata.h.
Refined ftCommonData and ftCo_DatAttrs typing: p_ftCommonData->x218 and x220 are floats, and ftCo_DatAttrs xB8 is a float.
Renamed vector macros from VEC2_DOT/VEC3_DOT to VEC2_SQ_LEN/VEC3_SQ_LEN and updated ftCo_Damage.c users, making clear these compute squared length rather than a dot product.
Added ftCo_SpecialS.c.o to obj_files.mk and adjusted tools/report_score.py to pass -f expected_path into asm-differ.
smart_moves: Split the side-special logic into a pure input predicate and an input-plus-enter function, preserving the behavior distinction shown by the original ft_800964FC and ft_80096540 asm.
Used the per-kind ftData_SpecialS function pointer table instead of hardcoding character behavior, matching the original dispatch pattern and making the common logic reusable across fighters.

Kept the C implementation small and structurally close to the removed asm: null callback check, x688 debounce/cooldown check, reverse-facing branch, ground-friction adjustment, then callback dispatch.
Introduced narrowly scoped headers rather than leaving unrelated 0961xx declarations in ft_0877.h or placeholder headers.
Used a MUST_MATCH-only unused stack byte array in doEnter to satisfy matching without polluting non-matching builds.
Corrected ambiguous data types to floats only where the decompiled expression needed arithmetic semantics, especially p_ftCommonData thresholds and co_attrs.xB8.
Renamed the VEC*_DOT macros to VEC*_SQ_LEN, preventing future decomp readers from mistaking squared magnitude calculations for vector dot products.
Included the score report in the PR body showing ftCo_SpecialS.c, ftCo_SpecialS_CheckInput, doEnter, and ftCo_SpecialS_HasInput all at 100%.
decomp_lessons: When extracting common fighter input handlers from asm, preserve both the 'has input' helper and the 'check input and transition' helper if the original code uses both; they may participate in different state timers or debounce fields.

Address-named functions should be renamed at callsites as soon as a confident behavioral name exists; this PR replaced ft_80096540 with ftCo_SpecialS_CheckInput across Wait, GuardOff, AttackS4, Link boomerang handling, Samus bomb handling, and asm call labels.

Function pointer arrays such as ftData_SpecialS are often the cleanest way to express common-to-character transitions; expose them in ftdata.h before trying to duplicate character-specific control flow.
Field typing is part of matching: changing ftCommonData x218/x220 and ftCo_DatAttrs xB8 to float enabled natural threshold/friction expressions instead of opaque integer/UNK usage.
Prefer semantic helper macro names: a macro that squares vector components is a squared-length macro, not a dot product macro.
Header cleanup can be necessary collateral for matching: moving declarations from broad ft_0877.h/ft_08A4.h into ftCo_0961.h and ftCo_SpecialS.h reduced placeholder leakage.
Use MUST_MATCH guards for artificial stack artifacts so the source remains readable in normal builds while still matching compiler output.
Updating the build list in obj_files.mk is a required part of asm-to-C extraction; otherwise the old asm object remains the source of truth.
naming_conventions: Common fighter action helpers use the ftCo_ prefix plus action name and behavior, e.g. ftCo_SpecialS_HasInput and ftCo_SpecialS_CheckInput.
A static helper can use a short internal verb phrase such as doEnter when it is local to the action implementation.
New action headers mirror the action source name: ftCo_SpecialS.c pairs with ftCo_SpecialS.h.
Address-based header guards continue to use GALE01_<address>, shown by GALE01_095FEC for ftCo_SpecialS.h and GALE01_09614C for ftCo_0961.h.

Unknown or not-yet-named nearby functions remain address-based in the focused header, e.g. ft_8009665C, ft_800968C8, ft_80096900, while known action callbacks use names such as ftCo_FallSpecial_Anim/IASA/Phys/Coll.
Vector squared-length macros should be named VEC2_SQ_LEN and VEC3_SQ_LEN rather than VEC2_DOT/VEC3_DOT.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Replaced 117 lines of asm in ft_08A4.s with a 49-line C implementation while retaining branch-equivalent behavior for input tests, callback null tests, timer tests, turn-around logic, and friction math.
The original absolute-value comparison against zero in ft_800964FC was expressed naturally as ABS(fp->input.lstick.x) >= p_ftCommonData->x218.
The original side-special entry's function pointer load from ftData_SpecialS[kind] became ftData_SpecialS[fp->kind](gobj).
The fneg/fmadds friction sequence was represented as fp->gr_vel += -(fp->gr_vel * (1 - fp->co_attrs.xB8)) * ft_GetGroundFrictionMultiplier(fp).
The doEnter helper carries a MUST_MATCH-only unused u8 stack array to account for an unused stack artifact in the matched function.
The diff also renamed branch labels/calls in remaining asm from ft_80096540 to ftCo_SpecialS_CheckInput so asm-side references stayed coherent after symbol naming.
tools/report_score.py was updated to pass -f expected_path to asm-differ, indicating the matching report tooling needed an explicit expected object/file path for this workflow.
review_feedback: No issue comments, review comments, or reviews were present in the provided PR slice; there is no recorded reviewer feedback to extract.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that rename or further decompile ft_8009665C, ft_800968C8, ft_80096900, or ft_800969D8 from ftCo_0961.h.
Search for subsequent cleanup of ftData_SpecialAirHi and ftData_SpecialAirLw declarations added beside ftData_SpecialS.
Check whether the ftdata.h comment typo around ftData_OnDeath was later corrected.
Find other common special input PRs that reuse the HasInput vs CheckInput split introduced here.
Search for remaining uses of ft_80096540 or ft_800964FC after this PR to verify full migration.
